questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with the fundamental rights?
questions.explanation: Part III contains the fundamental rights.

questions.question: What is the name of the highest court in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It is the Calcutta High Court.

questions.question: Who is the head of the state in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Governor is the head of the state.

questions.question: What is the name of the committee that drafted the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The B.N. Rau Committee drafted the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: What is the term of office for a member of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: A member of the Lok Sabha has a 5-year term.

questions.question: Who is the leader of the Indian National Congress in the Bengal Provincial Congress Session of 1920?
questions.explanation: Subhas Chandra Bose led the Bengal Provincial Congress.

questions.question: The Tebhaga Movement, a peasants' struggle, was primarily held in which district of Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Tebhaga Movement was held in 24 Parganas.

questions.question: Which of the following freedom fighters was associated with the Jugantar group in Bengal?
questions.explanation: Barindra Kumar Ghosh was a key figure in the Jugantar group.

questions.question: The famous 'Deshabandhu' title was given to?
questions.explanation: Jatindra Nath Mukherjee was known as 'Deshabandhu'.

questions.question: Which of the following revolutionary groups was founded by Bhupendranath Datta in Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Anushilan Samiti was founded by Bhupendranath Datta.

questions.question: Which river forms the northern border of the Indian state of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It is the major river flowing through the state.

questions.question: Which mountain range runs along the northern part of the Indian state of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It covers the northern part of the state.

questions.question: Which delta is the largest delta in India and located in the Indian state of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: Which region is known for its tea plantations located in the Indian state of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It is famous for Darjeeling tea.

questions.question: Which river forms the border between the Indian state of West Bengal and the neighboring country of Bangladesh?
questions.explanation: It is an international border.

questions.question: The West Bengal government recently launched a new initiative to improve air quality in Kolkata. What is the name of this initiative?
options: Paschim Banga O Samaj Suraksha Mission
questions.explanation: It aims to reduce air pollution in Kolkata.

questions.question: Which Indian city has been selected as the host city for the 2027 Asian Athletics Championships, and it is also in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Bhubaneswar was the wrong answer, actually, Bhubaneswar is in Odisha.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers flows through the Darjeeling hills in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It passes through the Darjeeling Himalayan hill region.

questions.question: The 2025 West Bengal Legislative Assembly election was a major success for which political party?
questions.explanation: They won the election with a landslide victory.

questions.question: The Indian government has recently sanctioned a new railway line to connect the port city of Haldia to the town of Tapan in West Bengal. What is the name of this proposed railway line?
questions.explanation: It aims to boost trade and commerce in the region.

questions.question: A shopkeeper marks up the price of a saree by 25% in Kolkata. If the marked price is Rs. 750, then what is the cost price of the saree?
questions.explanation: Cost price is obtained by dividing the marked price by 1.25.

questions.question: A businessman buys 1000 shares of Hindustan Motors at Rs. 120 each. He sells 1000 shares at Rs. 180 each. What is his profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Profit percentage is obtained by finding the difference between selling price and cost price, dividing it by cost price, and multiplying by 100.

questions.question: If the population of Hooghly district increases by 20% in a year, and the population was 1,230,000 in the previous year, then what is the population of Hooghly district in the new year?
questions.explanation: Population is obtained by multiplying the previous year's population by 1.20.

questions.question: A person invests Rs. 50,000 in a business. If he makes a profit of 15% after 1 year, then what is his profit amount?
questions.explanation: Profit amount is obtained by multiplying the investment by the profit percentage.

questions.question: A dealer marks up the price of a TV by 35% in West Bengal. If the cost price of the TV is Rs. 10,000, then what is the marked price?
questions.explanation: Marked price is obtained by multiplying the cost price by 1.35.

questions.question: A person can finish a piece of work in 12 hours. His brother helps him, and they complete the work in 4 hours. How long will brother alone take to complete the work?
questions.explanation: The brother alone will take 8 hours to complete the work.

questions.question: A man can fill a tank in 8 hours. Due to leakage, it takes 12 hours to fill the tank. In how many hours can the man fill the tank if the leakage is plugged?
questions.explanation: The man can fill the tank in 8 hours if the leakage is plugged.

questions.question: A well with a capacity of 240 litres can be filled by pipes A and B together in 12 hours. Pipe A can fill the well in 20 hours, while pipe B can fill it in 18 hours. How long will it take for pipe B to fill the well alone?
questions.explanation: Pipe B alone will take 18 hours to fill the well.

questions.question: A group of workers can complete a piece of work in 10 days. If 5 more workers are added to the group, in how many days will the work be completed?
questions.explanation: The work will be completed in 7.5 days with 5 more workers.

questions.question: A cistern can be filled by a pipe A in 10 hours, and by pipe B in 12 hours. If both pipes are opened simultaneously, in how many hours will the cistern be filled?
questions.explanation: The cistern will be filled in 9 hours if both pipes are opened simultaneously.

questions.question: A car covers a distance of 60 km in 1 hour 30 minutes. Its speed in km/h is:
questions.explanation: Speed is calculated in km/h.

questions.question: A boat covers a certain distance downstream in 2 hours and the same distance upstream in 4 hours. The speed of the boat in still water is:
questions.explanation: Speed of boat in still water is calculated by averaging downstream and upstream speeds.

questions.question: A man covers a distance of 12 km in 2 hours with a certain part of the distance covered on foot and the remaining part covered by bus. If the speed of the bus is 30 km/h, the distance covered on foot is:
questions.explanation: Time taken and speed of the bus are used to calculate the distance covered.

questions.question: A train travels from Howrah to Bhubaneswar at an average speed of 60 km/h and returns back to Howrah at an average speed of 40 km/h. The average speed of the train for the entire trip is:
questions.explanation: Average speed is calculated using harmonic mean formula.

questions.question: A cyclist covers a distance of 24 km in 2 hours. The cyclist's speed is:
questions.explanation: Speed is calculated as distance divided by time.

questions.question: Amar is the son of the brother of the father of Babul. Who is Amar's mother?
questions.explanation: Amar's mother is Babul's wife.

questions.question: If Rukmini is the mother of Sunita, Sunita's paternal grandmother is the wife of?
questions.explanation: Rukmini's husband is Sunita's paternal grandfather.

questions.question: Dipankar is the son of the son of the father of Kalyan. What is the relationship of Dipankar with Kalyan?
questions.explanation: Dipankar is the brother of Kalyan.

questions.question: If the mother of Prasenjit is the sister of the father of Sourav, what is the relationship of Prasenjit with Sourav?
questions.explanation: Prasenjit is the cousin of Sourav.

questions.question: Kumari is the wife of the son of the mother of Pradip. Who is Kumari's father-in-law?
questions.explanation: Kumari's husband is the son of Pradip's mother.

questions.question: Kolkata's Victoria Memorial is facing eastwards, if you walk 150 meters north from it and then 200 meters east, which direction will you face?
questions.explanation: Direction will be south-east.

questions.question: You are in Siliguri, if you walk 400 meters east from your current location and then 350 meters north, which direction will you face?
questions.explanation: Direction will be south.

questions.question: Assam's border is located 50 kilometers north of Darjeeling, if you walk 25 kilometers south from there, which direction will you face?
questions.explanation: Direction will be south.

questions.question: You are on a road in Malda, if you walk 1.5 kilometers north and then 2 kilometers west, which direction will you face?
questions.explanation: Direction will be south.

questions.question: A location in Jalpaiguri has a temple facing west, if you walk 250 meters to the south of it and then 200 meters to the west, which direction will the west-facing temple be from your current location?
questions.explanation: Direction will be east.

questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ___
questions.explanation: This series follows a pattern of adding consecutive even numbers.

questions.question: Identify the correct pattern in the given series: D, E, G, I, K, ___
questions.explanation: This series follows an alphabetical pattern with each letter shifted by two positions.

questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ___
questions.explanation: This series consists of perfect squares of consecutive natural numbers.

questions.question: Identify the correct pattern in the given series: C, F, I, L, O, ___
questions.explanation: This series follows an alphabetical pattern with each letter shifted by three positions.

questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, ___
questions.explanation: This series consists of consecutive multiples of 3.
